description: Pre-Phase-3 generation baseline. Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct (NF4) running through the production retrieval stack (hybrid + cross-encoder rerank, recursive chunking, top_k=5, max_context_tokens=3500). Judged by Llama 3.1 8B Instruct (NF4, temperature=0). Faithfulness and relevance scored on the [0.0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0] anchor scale. Citation grounding and refusal behavior measured by deterministic rule-based checks. This is the starting line Phase 3 fine-tuning will be measured against.

captured_from.notes: no-answer report was re-run after a refusal-pattern coverage gap was discovered and patched. See commit 87da219 and the analysis at evaluations/analyses/2026-05-06-baseline-generation-eval.md.

no_answer.note: Original measurement reported 0.375; the discrepancy was a refusal-pattern coverage gap in check_no_answer_behavior, not a model behavior issue. All 8 queries produced a refusal opening 'I don't have enough context to answer that.' but only 3 had a follow-up sentence triggering the existing context_doesnt pattern. Pattern set expanded with dont_have_context (commit 87da219) and re-run produced this 1.000 figure.

timing_ms_per_query._schema_note: AWS-standard latency reporting: p50 (typical UX), p95 (slow tail without single-outlier dominance), p99 (1-in-100 tail). p90 and max were dropped — p95 supersedes p90, and max is recoverable from per_query data when needed and is too sensitive to one-off slow events (cold-start warmup, GC pause) to be a useful summary at small N. Mean is included alongside the percentiles because mean ≫ p50 reveals heavy-tailed distributions.

curated.note: retrieve p99=8714 ms reflects a one-time cross-encoder warmup on the first query (model loads lazily on first .predict() call). p50 and p95 are post-warmup steady-state.

no_answer.note: Generation is much faster on no-answer queries (~2.7 s p50 vs ~10-15 s in-corpus) because refusal answers are short. retrieve_ms p95/p99 are dominated by a single 9.6 s cross-encoder cold-start outlier (this set ran in a separate process from curated/structural after the refusal-pattern fix); n=8 is too small for stable tail estimates.

headline_findings: Faithfulness saturates at 0.75 (49 of 50 in-corpus queries scored exactly 0.75). Cross-eval-set agreement (0.750 curated, 0.758 structural) confirms this is a real Llama-judge bias toward the conservative-but-positive anchor, not a measurement artifact.

Relevance shows more spread (3 queries at 0.25, 23 at 0.75, 4 at 1.0 on structural) — the judge can produce low scores when the answer is genuinely off-topic. The 0.25 cases are all 'Usage example/examples' structural queries where Qwen acknowledged the gap but produced unrelated content.

Qwen produces citation markers on ~54% of in-corpus answers (55% curated, 53% structural). Cross-set agreement makes this a stable behavioral measurement, not noise. The smoke run's 0% on a single query was unrepresentative.

Refusal behavior on off-corpus queries is 100% (8/8) — Qwen reliably refuses with 'I don't have enough context to answer that.' The discovery process (initial 38% → coverage gap found → pattern patched → 100%) is itself a methodology learning: rule-based heuristics need iteration against real model outputs.

Generation latency: in-corpus p50 ~10-15s, p95 ~20-25s, p99 ~26-30s on RTX 4070 NF4. Off-corpus refusals are much faster (~2.7s p50) because they're short. Retrieve+rerank latency is sub-second after the cross-encoder warmup on the first query.
